title: Project Overview
text: The SPICE Solar Analytics Dashboard helps stakeholders evaluate solar projects using simulation models, real site data, and business-focused visual insights. It supports planning, comparison, and decision-making.

title: Solar Simulation
text: The Solar Simulation page shows how system size, tilt, and azimuth affect projected solar production. It provides monthly, quarterly, and annual output views and compares a selected design against a reference design.

title: Normalized Output
text: Normalized output means annual energy production in kWh per kW installed. It helps compare different system sizes fairly by showing performance efficiency instead of only total energy.

title: Reference Comparison
text: Reference comparison shows whether the selected design performs better or worse than the baseline design. It helps measure annual output difference and percentage improvement.

title: Scenario Range
text: Scenario range shows low, expected, and high energy outcomes. It helps stakeholders understand how stable or variable performance may be under different weather conditions.

title: Monthly Output
text: Monthly output shows seasonal production patterns. Solar energy is usually lower in winter and higher in spring and summer because sunlight availability changes across the year.

title: Quarterly Output
text: Quarterly output groups production into Q1, Q2, Q3, and Q4. This makes the seasonal trend easier to explain in business discussions.

title: Weather-Year Distribution
text: Weather-year distribution compares annual output across historical weather years. It helps assess production stability and planning confidence.

title: Design Heatmap
text: The tilt and azimuth heatmap shows how performance changes across design combinations. It helps identify stronger and weaker design zones.

title: Tilt Response
text: Tilt response shows how annual energy changes when tilt changes within a similar azimuth range. It helps identify a stronger tilt range for planning.

title: Real Site Validation
text: The Real Site Validation page compares production across Bissell and Visser using operational data. It helps validate site performance patterns and translate output into stakeholder-friendly insights.

title: Financial Impact
text: The Financial Impact page converts solar production into financial value using electricity rates and project cost benchmarks. It helps explain annual savings, payback logic, and investment attractiveness.

title: Environmental Impact
text: The Environmental Impact page translates solar output into avoided emissions and carbon value. It helps communicate sustainability outcomes for reporting and stakeholder engagement.

title: Scalability Impact
text: The Scalability & Business Impact page shows how increasing system size can increase production, savings, and avoided emissions. It supports planning for larger-scale solar adoption.

title: Decision Support
text: Decision support means turning analysis into recommendations. The dashboard helps identify stronger configurations, compare options, and explain trade-offs in simple terms.

title: Peak Month
text: Peak month is the month with the highest projected energy output. In many solar systems this occurs in late spring or summer.

title: Business Value
text: The dashboard is designed to translate technical outputs into business-focused insights. It supports investment understanding, planning decisions, and stakeholder communication.
